rationale.R1.1: The artifact is a related-work + contribution section; the contribution claim ('hybrid index,' '1.3x speedup') and gap statement (memory vs. storage tradeoff) must be reconstructed and verified for precision and completeness.
rationale.R1.2: The three-directional taxonomy of recent work and the positioning of FastNav within it must be internally consistent; the logical chain from prior limitations to proposed solution must hold.
rationale.R1.3: The 1.3x speedup claim at same recall@10 on SIFT1B, Deep1B, BIGANN is a central empirical assertion; reviewing this section requires checking that the claim is adequately grounded and not overstated relative to what is presented.
rationale.R1.4: The core novelty claim — anisotropic compression combined with disk-resident traversal in a single hybrid index — must be assessed against ScaNN, DiskANN, and NSG to determine whether the delta is genuine and correctly characterized.
rationale.R1.6: As a prose section intended for publication, sentence-level clarity, precision of technical language, and paragraph cohesion are standard review targets.
rationale.R1.7: Five specific citations are named (Indyk & Motwani 1998, Jegou et al. 2011, Guo et al. 2020, Subramanya et al. 2019, Fu et al. 2019); accuracy of author lists, years, and attribution of specific contributions (e.g. 'theoretical guarantees on navigability' to NSG) must be verified.
